behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names all file paths, command name 'kill-all', the description phrase, category, toolName/toolParams behavior, function signature requirements, and docs sections tested.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what to implement but not exact code lines; agent must still reason about implementation structure.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert specific implementation details like 'const killAll = declareCommand({', 'async function killAllDaemons(): Promise<void>', 'await killAllDaemons()', and 'import { execSync, spawn } from 'child_process';' — alternative valid names (e.g. killAllDaemonProcesses, different import order) would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Except for test_typescript_compiles, all tests are string-in-file-content assertions; no execution of the kill-all command or its behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require actual code additions (function definition, imports, command declaration) that a stub would not satisfy.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover commands.ts declaration, program.ts handling, function implementation, and both doc files as required by instruction.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section headers (PASS-TO-PASS, FAIL-TO-PASS code, FAIL-TO-PASS docs) and descriptive names.

no_typos.reason: Paths and command names appear consistent between instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names files to modify but that's appropriate scope; doesn't point to buggy line numbers.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Doc edits in SKILL.md and session-management.md are localized additions, not full rewrites.
